ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03302051 maps to banana fritter; flavoured variant is a subtype not separately coded — exact match accepted at the fritter level.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers fried banana snack; flavoured toppings are not separately coded at FoodEx2 level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for banana fritter or goreng pisang as of 2026.

main_ingredients: flavouring agents (e.g. cheese powder, chocolate, pandan)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of flavoured goreng pisang is approximately 2 pieces (about 100 g), commonly sold at hawker centres and pasar malam stalls in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured Goreng Pisang — HPB Singapore Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All per_100g core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Flavoured Goreng Pisang (CR ID F11090012). Confidence 0.95 as reported in source metadata. saturated_fat_g and trans_fat_g are estimated from deep-frying profile as HPB entry does not separately report fatty acid breakdown.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — banana fritter / fried banana snack analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for flavoured goreng pisang. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for deep-fried banana fritter / banana in batter (medium GI range 60–70), consistent with the high sugar content (17.15 g/100g) and refined wheat flour batter. Glycemic load per 100 g serving = (65 × 31.27) / 100 = 20.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 604 mg/100 g exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold; combined with high sugar and saturated fat from deep-frying.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit flavoured goreng pisang due to sodium at 604 mg/100 g, which alone exceeds the hypertension sodium hard gate, and the additional cardiovascular burden of 22.44 g fat/100 g from deep-frying.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 17.15 g/100 g combined with medium-high GI (estimated 65) and refined batter creates a high glycaemic load of 20.3 per 100 g serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid flavoured goreng pisang because the high sugar content (17.15 g/100 g), medium-high GI (~65), and glycaemic load of 20.3 per serving will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation, worsened by saturated fat impairing insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat 22.44 g/100 g including estimated saturated fat ~4.5 g and trans fat ~0.5 g from deep-frying; sodium 604 mg/100 g further elevates cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid flavoured goreng pisang due to the combination of high total fat (22.44 g/100 g), estimated saturated fat (~4.5 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.5 g/100 g) from deep-frying, and sodium at 604 mg/100 g, all of which contribute to atherogenic and hypertensive cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that worsen joint inflammation; trans fat (~0.5 g/100 g) and sodium (604 mg/100 g) further exacerbate RA-related tissue swelling.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid flavoured goreng pisang because deep-frying generates pro-inflammatory AGEs, trans fat (~0.5 g/100 g) is strongly pro-inflammatory, and sodium at 604 mg/100 g worsens RA-related tissue swelling and systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured goreng pisang is a popular Singaporean hawker snack consisting of ripe bananas coated in a seasoned wheat flour batter and deep-fried until golden and crispy, then topped or dusted with added flavourings such as cheese powder, chocolate sauce, pandan, or other sweet and savoury coatings. It is a modern evolution of the classic goreng pisang (plain banana fritter) that has become widely sold at pasar malam night markets, hawker centres, and roadside stalls across Singapore. The texture is crispy on the outside with a soft, sweet banana interior. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 339 kcal, 22.44 g fat, 31.27 g carbohydrate, 17.15 g sugar, 3.03 g protein, 2.46 g dietary fibre, and 604 mg sodium. The high sodium content (604 mg/100 g) exceeds the hypertension threshold, the high sugar (17.15 g/100 g) combined with a medium-high estimated GI of ~65 creates a glycaemic load of ~20.3 per serving, and the deep-frying process contributes saturated and trans fats. This food is not suitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is classified as a processed snack with high fat, high sodium, and high sugar warnings. Halal-suitable in most preparations; contains gluten from wheat flour batter.
common_user_queries: How many calories in flavoured banana fritter?
Is flavoured goreng pisang high in sodium?
What is the GI of goreng pisang?
Can I eat goreng pisang if I have high blood pressure?
What flavours does goreng pisang come in?
How much sugar is in goreng pisang?
Is banana fritter good for weight loss?
